name: Semaglutide
description: GLP-1 receptor agonist for metabolic research and appetite regulation studies

overview.what_is: Semaglutide (Wegovy®, Ozempic®) is a human GLP-1 analogue of 31 amino acids with 94% homology, modified with an Ala→Aib substitution at position 8 and conjugation with C18 fatty acid via spacer, providing a half-life of ~7 days for weekly administration. Mechanism: Pure and potent agonism of GLP-1R — glucose-dependent insulin secretion, glucagon inhibition, gastric emptying delay, and central hypothalamic action in appetite suppression. In the STEP-1 trial (NEJM 2021), 2.4mg/week produced a 14.9% weight reduction in 68 weeks. Titration Protocol Step Dosage Step 1 0.25mg/week (4 weeks) Step 2 0.50mg/week (≥4 weeks) Step 3 1.0mg/week (≥4 weeks) Step 4 1.7mg/week (≥4 weeks) Target— 2.4mg/week (maintenance) Post-reconstitution validity— 30 days Step 5 (refrigerator 2-8°C). Cycle— max 16 weeks ON / 4 weeks OFF.

protocols.reconstitution: Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store at 2-8°C.

research.mechanism: Semaglutide is a GLP-1 analogue with 94% homology to human GLP-1, modified with a C18 fatty acid side chain that binds to serum albumin, extending its half-life to approximately 7 days. It activates the GLP-1 receptor (GLP-1R) on pancreatic beta cells, stimulating glucose-dependent insulin secretion and suppressing glucagon secretion. In the central nervous system, it acts on hypothalamic nuclei and the brainstem to reduce appetite and caloric intake. Peripherally, it delays gastric emptying, contributing to prolonged satiety. Its beneficial cardiovascular effects include reduced vascular inflammation and improved endothelial function.

name: Cagrilintide
description: Long-acting amylin analogue for appetite regulation and metabolic research

overview.what_is: Cagrilintide is a long-acting acylated analogue of the amylin hormone, which acts as an amylin receptor agonist. Its main function is to promote satiety and delay gastric emptying, resulting in reduced food intake and weight loss. This synthetic peptide was developed to mimic the physiological effects of endogenous amylin.

research.mechanism: Cagrilintide is a human amylin analogue with chemical modifications (C18 fatty acid acylation) that drastically prolong its half-life, allowing for weekly dosing. It activates amylin receptors (AMY1-3), which are heterodimers of the calcitonin receptor with RAMP proteins. In the CNS, particularly in the area postrema and nucleus of the solitary tract, receptor activation reduces appetite and promotes satiety. Peripherally, it slows gastric emptying, suppressing the postprandial glucagon secretion. The combination with GLP-1 agonists (such as semaglutide) has shown additive weight loss as they act on complementary pathways for appetite control.

overview.what_is: AOD-9604 is a synthetic peptide, a modified fragment of the C-terminal region of human growth hormone (hGH 177-191), designed to promote lipolysis and inhibit lipogenesis without the systemic metabolic effects of intact hGH. Its action focuses on fat mobilization, distinguishing it from other hGH analogs by not elevating IGF-1 levels or inducing insulin resistance.

research.mechanism: AOD-9604 is a modified fragment of hGH that retains the lipolytic activity of growth hormone without its anabolic and diabetogenic effects. Unlike intact hGH, AOD-9604 does not stimulate IGF-1 production or affect glucose metabolism, which could ensure a superior safety profile for use in obesity management.

research.mechanism: NNMT is an enzyme that methylates nicotinamide, consuming SAM (S-adenosylmethionine) in the process. In adipose tissues of obese individuals, NNMT is overexpressed, which reduces SAM and NAD+ levels, compromising cellular energy metabolism. 5-Amino-1MQ selectively inhibits this enzyme, restoring SAM and NAD+ levels. With more NAD+ available, sirtuins (especially SIRT1) are activated, promoting fatty acid oxidation, mitochondrial biogenesis, and thermogenesis. This dual mechanism — SAM preservation and NAD+ elevation — creates a favorable metabolic environment for fat loss without muscle catabolism, distinguishing it from simple thermogenics.

Increases thermogenesis without muscle catabolism

research.mechanism: SS-31 has a selective affinity for cardiolipin (CL), a unique phospholipid of the inner mitochondrial membrane. CL is essential for the organization of respiratory chain complexes into supercomplexes. With aging, CL undergoes oxidation and peroxidation, destabilizing the electron chain. SS-31 anchors to CL, preventing its oxidation and restoring the efficiency of complexes I, III, and IV. This results in a 20-30% increase in ATP production and up to a 60% reduction in ROS generation. Unlike conventional antioxidants, SS-31 does not indiscriminately eliminate ROS, preserving the redox signaling necessary for cellular adaptations.

name: HGH Fragment 176-191
description: GH fragment for fat metabolism research without growth effects

overview.what_is: HGH Fragment 176-191 is a synthetic fragment composed of the amino acids 176 to 191 of the human growth hormone (hGH) chain. This specific segment corresponds to the region responsible for the lipolytic activity of GH — that is, fat burning — but without the undesirable side effects of full GH, such as hyperglycemia, water retention, or acromegaly. Research has shown that HGH Frag 176-191 is up to 12.5 times more potent than full GH in inducing lipolysis, making it the most effective peptide available for targeted fat burning. It stimulates lipolysis (fat breakdown) and inhibits lipogenesis (formation of new fat cells), without affecting IGF-1 or insulin levels.

overview.what_is:
FOXO4-DRI is a next-generation senolytic peptide that selectively eliminates senescent cells ('zombie cells'). It interferes with the FOXO4-p53 interaction, forcing senescent cells into apoptosis without affecting healthy cells. In animal studies, it resulted in dramatic improvements in fur density, kidney function, physical strength, and overall health.

Proven Benefits
1 Elimination of senescent cells throughout the body
2 Reduction of chronic systemic inflammation (inflammaging)

3 Improvement of organ function (kidneys, liver)
4 Reversal of physical signs of aging
5 Increased physical vitality and endurance
Results Timeline
Days 1-3 Possible fatigue or flu-like symptoms (Herxheimer reaction).
Weeks 1-2 Recovery and onset of effects.
Months 1-2 Improvement in energy, skin, and organ function.
Months 3-6 Cumulative anti-aging benefits.

The peptide competes with FOXO4, releasing p53 to signal programmed cell death. Healthy cells, which do not rely on this interaction for survival, are not affected.

name: Cerebrolysin
description: Injectable formulation of neurotrophic peptide factors.

overview.what_is:
Cerebrolysin is a biotechnological peptide mixture derived from purified brain proteins from pigs. It contains low molecular weight neuropeptides that mimic BDNF, GDNF, CNTF, and NGF. It is one of the most clinically validated treatments for ischemic stroke, vascular dementia, Alzheimer's disease, and traumatic brain injuries.

Proven Benefits
1 Accelerates recovery post-stroke and traumatic brain injury
2 Improves memory, attention, and cognition in the elderly

3 Protects against neurodegenerative decline
4 Enhances mood and mental clarity
5 Stimulates regeneration of peripheral nerves
Results Timeline
Day 1-3 Sensation of mental clarity and subtle improvement.
Week 1-2 Improvement in memory and processing speed.
Week 3-4 Consolidated cognitive benefits.
Month 2-3 Long-term neuroprotection and regeneration.

reconstitution_steps: Comes ready in sterile glass ampoules
Use immediately after opening
Do not mix in the syringe with other compounds
Store at room temperature protected from light
protocols.reconstitution: Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store at 2-8°C.

research.mechanism: Cerebrolysin acts through a multimodal mechanism: it protects neurons against oxidative stress and glutamate excitotoxicity; stimulates neuroplasticity and the formation of new synapses; enhances cerebral energy metabolism; and inhibits the formation of amyloid plaques and neurofibrillary tangles. Its neuropeptides mimic the action of endogenous neurotrophic factors.
steps: Protection against oxidative stress and glutamate excitotoxicity
Stimulation of neuroplasticity and synaptogenesis
Improvement of cerebral energy metabolism

name: Cartalax
description: Bioregulator peptide for cartilage and joint health.

overview.what_is:
Cartalax is a synthetic bioregulator tripeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp) that modulates gene expression in chondrocytes, promoting the synthesis of extracellular matrix components such as type II collagen and aggrecan. Its main function is the protection and regeneration of articular cartilage, with anti-aging properties that act on the preservation of tissue integrity.

Proven Benefits
1 Protection and regeneration of articular cartilage
2 Improvement of joint function and mobility

3 Cellular anti-aging properties
4 Modulation of gene expression in chondrocytes
5 Support for musculoskeletal health
Results Timeline
Week 1-2 Minimal effects noticeable; compound accumulating
Week 3-4 Subtle improvement in joint mobility.
Month 2-3 More pronounced benefits in joint health.

name: Cardiogen
description: Bioregulator peptide for cardiac muscle and cardiovascular research

overview.what_is: Cardiogen is a synthetic bioregulatory tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Arg) that acts as a cardioprotective and cell-repairing agent. It modulates intracellular survival pathways, promoting cytoskeletal integrity and inhibiting apoptosis in stressed cardiomyocytes.

protocols.title: Standard / Gradual Protocol (3 mL = ~6.67 mg/mL)
reconstitution_steps: Aspirate 3.0 mL of bacteriostatic water with a sterile syringe
Inject slowly into the side of the vial; avoid foaming
Gently swirl until dissolved (do not shake)
Label and refrigerate at 2–8°C, protect from light
protocols.reconstitution: Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store at 2-8°C.

research.mechanism: Cardiogen, like other bioregulatory peptides developed by Prof. Khavinson, acts at the level of epigenetic regulation of gene expression. The tetrapeptide Ala-Glu-Asp-Arg binds to DNA at specific promoter regions of genes related to cardiac function, modulating the transcription of essential proteins for cardiomyocyte integrity. This includes anti-apoptotic proteins (Bcl-2), antioxidant enzymes (SOD, catalase), and components of the cytoskeleton. Additionally, it regulates intracellular calcium homeostasis, which is crucial for proper cardiac contraction, and protects the cell membrane from oxidative damage. These combined effects confer cardioprotection and promote cellular repair under ischemic stress conditions.
steps: Modulation of gene expression of proteins involved in integrity
Activation of anti-apoptotic signaling pathways.
Stabilization of the cell membrane and cytoskeleton.
Regulation of intracellular calcium homeostasis.
Increase of endogenous antioxidant enzyme activity.

name: Adamax
description: Nootropic peptide derived from Semax with advanced neuroprotective properties.

overview.what_is: Adamax is a nootropic and neuroprotective heptapeptide, a modified analogue of Semax, derived from the ACTH(4-10) fragment. Its structure incorporates an adamantane group, which optimizes blood-brain barrier penetration and increases the metabolic stability of the peptide, potentiating its cognitive and neuronal protective effects.
overview.mechanism_summary: Adamax acts as an enhanced analogue of Semax, with the addition of an adamantane group that confers greater lipophilicity and metabolic stability. This allows better penetration of the blood-brain barrier, resulting in greater cerebral bioavailability. Once in the CNS, it modulates multiple neurotransmitter systems including dopaminergic and serotonergic pathways, as well as mechanisms for neuronal survival, synaptic plasticity, and neurogenesis.

name: Dihexa
description: Angiotensin IV-derived peptide with potent nootropic effects.

overview.what_is:
Dihexa is a small peptide derived from angiotensin IV, considered one of the most potent nootropics ever discovered. With the ability to promote synaptogenesis orders of magnitude greater than natural BDNF, it acts via activation of the HGF/c-Met pathway to repair and create new neural connections. Developed for Alzheimer’s, it is reserved for significant cognitive decline.

Proven Benefits
1 Potential to reverse cognitive damage and memory loss
2 Stimulates massive creation of new neural connections

3 Drastic improvement in learning and retention
4 Neuroprotection against degenerative diseases
5 Improvement in motor coordination and executive function
Result Timeline
Day 1-3 Onset of subtle nootropic effects.
Week 1-2 Noticeable improvement in mental clarity.
Week 3-4 Consolidated cognitive benefits.
Month 2-3 Synaptic integration and sustained results.

reconstitution_steps: Generally sold in capsules or powder
For topical use, requires DMSO as a solvent
Store in a cool, dry place
Protect from light and moisture
protocols.reconstitution: Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store at 2-8°C.

research.mechanism: Dihexa acts as an agonist of HGF and its receptor c-Met. In the brain, HGF/c-Met signaling is crucial for synaptic development, neuronal survival, and plasticity. Studies have shown it is 7 orders of magnitude more potent than BDNF in synapse formation, making it the most potent nootropic known.
steps: Agonism of Hepatocyte Growth Factor (HGF)
Activation of the c-Met receptor in the brain
Stimulation of dendritic spine formation
Strengthening of existing synaptic connections
